name: Sora
context: college senior; threads: graduation thesis, cafe part-time job, recent breakup

episodes.text: Met my thesis advisor for the first meeting of the semester. She approved my topic on urban heat islands and gave me a reading list of nine papers.

probes.query: How many papers were on the reading list my advisor gave me?
I feel steady and calm thinking about how my thesis journey began.
The moment a long project of mine got its starting signal drifts back sometimes.

episodes.text: Minho and I broke up in the parking lot behind the cafe after three years. He said we had become roommates with schedules, and the worst part is he was right.

probes.query: Where did Minho and I break up?
I still feel heartbroken when the breakup crosses my mind.
What ended behind that building three springs in the making still visits me at night.

episodes.text: Regular closing shift at the cafe. Wiped the machines, counted the till twice because I was off by five hundred won, restocked cups for the morning crew.

probes.query: Why did I count the till twice at closing?
I feel neutral, almost fond, about those routine closing shifts.
The end-of-day rhythm of that job hums back to me in quiet moments.

episodes.text: Deleted our shared photo album after backing it up to a drive I mailed to my sister. Two hundred and forty pictures, three years, one padded envelope.

probes.query: How many pictures were in the album I backed up and deleted?
I feel a wave of grief remembering the day I archived those photos.
Everything we collected fit into one small package, and that thought resurfaces.

episodes.text: Set up my thesis data pipeline: downloaded satellite temperature tiles for the city, wrote a cleaning script, and got the first heat map to render.

probes.query: What kind of data tiles did I download for the thesis pipeline?
I feel quietly satisfied about getting that first visualization working.
The night a picture of the whole town first appeared on my screen returns to me.

episodes.text: A customer screamed at me over a mistaken oat-milk charge and threw the receipt at my chest. The manager backed me up, but my hands shook while steaming milk for an hour.

probes.query: What did the angry customer throw at me?
I still feel shaken and humiliated by that customer incident.
One stranger's outburst at the counter left a mark that flares up on hard shifts.

episodes.text: Bought groceries, mended the hem of my work apron, and returned two library books a day before the due date. Small, checkable boxes.

probes.query: What did I mend along with running errands that day?
I feel calm recalling that plain day of small errands.
A short list of little duties, all crossed off, floats through my head sometimes.

episodes.text: My survey went live and collected one hundred and eighty responses in the first week, double what my advisor expected. She wrote back a single line: now the fun begins.

probes.query: How many responses did my survey collect in the first week?
I feel a rush of excitement remembering how the survey took off.
The week strangers showed up for my questions in numbers I never imagined stays vivid.

episodes.text: New espresso blend arrived at the cafe and we ran taste tests between orders. I preferred the darker roast; the morning crew outvoted me for the lighter one.

probes.query: Which roast did I prefer in the cafe taste test?
I feel lightly amused remembering losing that coffee vote.
A tiny workplace debate over flavors resurfaces whenever I brew at home.

episodes.text: Ran into Minho at the station with someone new. We nodded like distant classmates, and I cried on the platform after his train left, then bought myself flowers on the walk home.

probes.query: What did I buy myself on the walk home from the station?
I still feel that stab of sadness from running into my ex that evening.
A brief nod in passing undid me once, and the scene replays on every commute.

episodes.text: Advisor tore apart my methods chapter draft: eleven comments, two entire sections to rewrite. Fair points, but I closed the laptop and stared at the wall for a while.

probes.query: How many comments did my advisor leave on the methods chapter?
I feel discouraged all over again thinking of that harsh draft feedback.
The red ink all over something I had labored on comes back before every submission.

episodes.text: Coworker Jia taught me latte art on the closing shift. My first three attempts looked like clouds, the fourth was almost a heart, and we laughed until the mop water went cold.

probes.query: What did my fourth latte art attempt almost look like?
I feel warm and happy remembering that latte art lesson with Jia.
An after-hours lesson in pouring shapes with a friend keeps bubbling up in me.

episodes.text: Submitted the rewritten methods chapter and updated my reference manager. Ninety-one citations now, all with page numbers, which past me would never believe.

probes.query: How many citations are in my reference manager now?
I feel a calm pride about resubmitting that chapter.
Handing back a piece of work I rebuilt from its bones lingers with me.

episodes.text: Quiet shift, rain outside. I laminated the new menu cards, refilled the syrup pumps, and watched umbrellas pass the window between customers.

probes.query: What did I laminate during the quiet rainy shift?
I feel peaceful thinking about that slow rainy evening at work.
A slow, wet evening watching the street from behind the counter drifts back to me.
